description: ALPHA: subject to change. Ephemeral live-performance state for an actor. This record is STATE, not attestation. It is the sole record in the social.skaists.alpha.performance.* namespace that is mutable in place, and it carries no historical claim about anything. Sets are append-only; status is overwritten. Distinct collection from fm.teal.alpha.actor.status, so a performer broadcasting a live set never clobbers their own personal scrobbling status.

main.description: One live status per actor. A performer plays one set at a time. Back-to-back performers each hold their own status.

updatedAt.description: RFC 3339 time of the most recent change to this record.

startedAt.description: RFC 3339 time the live performance began.

expiry.description: RFC 3339 time past which this status is stale. When absent, consumers MUST default to 10 minutes past updatedAt — convention borrowed from fm.teal.alpha.actor.status. A stale status MUST NOT be rendered as live. See invariant STATUS-2.

setKind.description: Open vocabulary. Unrecognized values preserved verbatim, rendered as unknown, never coerced to 'live'.

nowPlaying.description: The track currently playing. Optional: a performer may be live without disclosing the track.

performers.description: Ordered for b2b. When absent, the repository owner is the sole performer. MUST be present and complete whenever any performer is non-human. See invariant SET-11.

eventUri.description: Unpinned reference to a community.lexicon.calendar.event record. Same semantics as social.skaists.alpha.performance.set#eventUri: presence, not description.

setUri.description: Unpinned reference to the social.skaists.alpha.performance.set record for this performance, once one exists. A live status MUST NOT be promoted into a set by mutation; the set is written separately, once, when the performance is over. See invariant STATUS-3.

streamUrl.description: Where the performance can be heard live, if anywhere.
